When DML error logging is turned on for a table, Snowflake automatically creates an error table associated with the base table. DML operations that encounter supported errors log the errors in the error table instead of failing. Only errors directly related to the base table are logged, such as NOT NULL constraint violations, type conversion errors, incompatible precision and scale values, incompatible length for string and binary types, and some expression evaluation failures. The error table can be queried or truncated, and only the owner or users with the SELECT ERROR TABLE privilege can access it.
